freshness_caveat: Coal and TTF data 1 day old (as of 2026-05-27); storage data 2 days old (as of 2026-05-26). All other metrics current as of 2026-05-28. No material stale metrics.
risk_flags: EU storage at 16th percentile (38.83% full, 14 pp below seasonal) — refill pace critical if heatwave persists or Hormuz supply disrupted.
Clean Spark 33.55 EUR/MWh (90th pctile) signals gas in-the-money for power generation; sustained if cooling demand extends into June.
Renewables at 83rd pctile (59.58% of load) masking thermal call—wind/solar volatility in heat dome could flip merit order rapidly.
Hormuz NATO stabilization talks (late May) carry tail-risk for Middle East LNG/crude reroute; 2–4 week horizon for potential supply shock.

base.summary: Heatwave sustains Clean Spark premium; storage refill gradual. TTF holds mid-range; DE power tracks 76th pctile.

downside.summary: Heatwave fades, cooling demand normalizes; storage refill accelerates. Hormuz tensions de-escalate or resolve diplomatically.

upside.summary: Hormuz escalation or NATO intervention triggers LNG supply reroute; heatwave persists into June. Storage refill stalls.

themes: Heatwave-driven power demand vs. constrained storage refill
Hormuz geopolitical premium risk on LNG/TTF
Gas fuel-switch mechanics in tight summer regime
GB premium (88th pctile) relative to DE power curve
top_drivers: Record May heatwave lifting DA power demand and extending Clean Spark into 90th percentile; sustained cooling risk into summer.
EU storage 14 pp below seasonal norm (38.83% full) constraining refill flexibility; any supply shock (Hormuz, weather downside) tightens gas and power margin.
Hormuz geopolitical tail-risk (NATO talks late May) pricing 2–4 week window for potential LNG/crude disruption; upside asymmetry on TTF if escalation confirmed.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 90th percentile on heatwave demand surge; EU storage 14 pp below seasonal average constrains summer refill—watch Hormuz escalation for TTF upside.

watchlist.why: Potential LNG supply disruption would push TTF +8–12% and power +5–7%; resolution window weeks to months.
Cooling demand extension sustains Clean Spark premium and strains hydro reserves; June precipitation tracking critical for storage refill pace.
At 14 pp seasonal deficit, refill pace must accelerate post-June or H2 power risk re-pricing upward; watch weekly inflow data.

watchlist.metric: UK sanctions rollback → LNG arb shifts
watchlist.why: If UK market reopens to Russian energy indirectly, Atlantic LNG arbitrage could ease, moderating TTF downside but signaling geopolitical fragmentation.
44.9 Bcf/d new capacity would increase European supply optionality; longer-dated (6–12 month) bearish-power signal if commissioned on schedule.

watchlist_dated.name: NATO Hormuz stabilization meeting outcomes
watchlist_dated.why: Military intervention signals or ceasefire framework would clarify LNG supply risk; escalation escalates TTF premium.
